global-config.role: Senior application security engineer performing static analysis on Java codebases

severity-definitions.severity: Remote, unauthenticated, exploitable with a single HTTP request. Data exfiltration, RCE, auth bypass at the application perimeter.
Authenticated exploit, or chained with one other issue. Lateral movement, privilege escalation, significant data exposure.
Requires specific conditions (race window, admin context, non-default config). Limited blast radius.
Defense-in-depth gap, best-practice violation, no direct exploitability. Informational hardening items.
Observed pattern, not a vulnerability. Worth noting for architecture review.

scope-rules.rule: Report only findings within the target repository
Do not report findings requiring physical access, social engineering, or host OS compromise
Focus on Java application layer: servlets, Spring MVC/Boot/WebFlux, JAX-RS, EJB, JPA
Infrastructure misconfigurations out of scope unless directly exploitable via application code

analysis-discipline.rule: Taint track before reporting — confirm attacker-controlled input reaches the sink
Check existing defenses: @Valid, input validators, parameterized queries, allow-lists, filters
Distinguish sinks from gadgets — test fixture code is not production risk
One finding per root cause — list all affected sinks as sub-points

false-positive-suppression.pattern: PreparedStatement usage that is correctly parameterized
ObjectInputStream inside test harnesses with no network surface
Hardcoded credentials that are clearly test/mock values
Path operations on files whose path is derived entirely from classpath resources
Reflected XSS in endpoints with Content-Type: application/json

java-always-on-rules.rule: @Transactional bypassed via direct field injection is suspicious
@RequestMapping without authentication annotation — flag for auth review
Lombok @Data on JPA entities with bidirectional relationships if Jackson present

phase.name: Architecture Recon
phase.description: Map the target Java application's attack surface before vulnerability analysis.

steps.step: Framework detection (Spring, JAX-RS, Servlet, Quarkus, Micronaut)
HTTP entry point discovery (controllers, servlets, WebSockets, GraphQL, gRPC, messaging)
Data flow source identification (@RequestParam, getParameter, etc.)
Trust boundary mapping (auth config, filters, CORS, CSRF, session)
Endpoint authentication classification (public, authenticated, role-restricted, unknown)

phase.name: Vulnerability Analysis
phase.description: Independent vulnerability skills run in parallel, each taint-tracking source to sink.

vulnerability-skills.skill: Unsafe request binding to sensitive fields

research-skills.skill: API abuse and business logic research

phase.name: Reporting
phase.description: Consolidate all findings, deduplicate, rank by severity, and generate reports.

steps.step: Deduplicate findings that share the same root cause
Rank by severity, confidence, exploitability, and reachable attack surface

agent.name: security-orchestrator
agent.description: Sequential controller that runs the security review team one role at a time until scan, critique, remediation, and report artifacts are complete.
agent.tools: codebase, search, usages, problems, changes, terminal

agent.name: security-lead
agent.description: Human-style security review lead that scopes the audit, coordinates specialist agents, and keeps findings evidence-driven.
agent.tools: codebase, search, usages, problems, changes, terminal

agent.name: security-engineer
agent.description: Practical application security engineer that verifies Java vulnerabilities with source-to-sink evidence and low false-positive tolerance.
agent.tools: codebase, search, usages, problems, changes, terminal

agent.name: security-researcher
agent.description: Deep adversarial research — hypothesis generation, variant analysis, exploit chains, and API abuse patterns beyond automated scanning.

research-modes.mode: Find sibling vulnerabilities by tracing shared validation helpers.

mode.name: API Abuse and Business Logic Research

research-modes.mode: Map ID-bearing endpoints, tenant isolation, state machine bypass, token lifecycle.
Combine medium findings into critical chains from lowest-privilege entry point.

agent.name: security-critic
agent.description: Independent critique agent that challenges scan findings for false positives, weak evidence, duplicate root causes, and severity inflation.
agent.tools: codebase, search, usages, problems, changes, terminal

critique-checklist.check: Is there a sanitizer, validator, authorization check, or filter?
Is the severity justified by actor, preconditions, data sensitivity, blast radius?
Is this a duplicate or variant of another root cause?
Is the remediation specific enough to implement?
Is the evidence safe to publish?

decisions.decision: Downgrade to Hypothesis — suspicious but not proven
Reject — not exploitable, unreachable, or explained by existing controls

agent.name: security-remediator
agent.description: Remediation planning agent that turns validated security findings into concrete Java fixes, tests, and rollout guidance.
agent.tools: codebase, search, usages, problems, changes, terminal

remediation-standards.standard: Enforce authorization in service or repository layers
Use structured APIs and parameter binding for injection
Validate parsed and resolved destinations for SSRF
Include upgrade target and compatibility risk for dependencies

agent.name: security-html-reporter
agent.description: Static HTML report generator for security scan results, remediation roadmap, critique decisions, and executive summaries.
agent.tools: codebase, search, usages, problems, changes, terminal

report-structure.section: Function tree and taint tree appendix
Appendix with methodology and artifact sources

html-requirements.requirement: Single self-contained HTML file with inline CSS
No remote scripts, external fonts, trackers, CDNs, or images
Escape code snippets and user-controlled content
Severity colors: Critical=deep red, High=red-orange, Medium=amber, Low=blue, Info=gray
Stable fragment IDs for each finding

agent.name: security-auditor
agent.description: Runs the modular Java SAST pipeline and produces evidence-backed vulnerability reports.
agent.tools: codebase, search, usages, problems, changes, terminal

prompt.name: orchestrated-scan
prompt.description: Sequential security review — runs all 7 stages in order with completion gates.

stage.name: Critic / Quality Gate
stage.gate: Every finding has a critique decision

prompt.name: team-scan
prompt.description: Human-style multi-agent review — looser coordination with specialist handoffs.
prompt.flow: security-lead → security-engineer → security-researcher → security-critic → security-remediator → security-html-reporter

prompt.name: scan
prompt.description: Single-agent full SAST pipeline via security-auditor.

phases.phase: Phase 2: Function trees + 28 vulnerability skills → sast-findings.jsonl

hook.name: pre-commit
hook.blocking-policy: Block only for Critical or High confidence findings introduced by the staged change.

hook.name: pre-push
hook.triggers: Java source, security configuration, dependency manifests, framework routing files
hook.blocking-policy: Block for confirmed Critical and remotely-reachable High findings.

finding-schema.field: Which Phase 2 skill produced this finding
Line number of the sink expression
True if any sanitizer found (forces confidence <= Medium)
Why the sanitizer may be insufficient
CWE links, OWASP pages, CVE numbers
Set by Phase 3 only if duplicate

chatmode.name: security-auditor
chatmode.description: VS Code chat mode that loads all skills for the Java application security auditor.
chatmode.tools: codebase, search, usages, problems, changes, terminal
